etymology.description: 斷(斷) (duàn, "cut off") + 捨(捨) (shě, "discard") + 離(離) (lí, "separate").
etymology.interpretation: 斷(斷) (duàn, "cut off") involves decisively breaking away from attachments, typically physical or emotional. 捨(捨) (shě, "discard") suggests letting go of unnecessary items or burdens, emphasizing the action of removal. 離(離) (lí, "separate") implies creating distance from the clutter or allure of material possessions. Together, 斷捨離 (duànshělí) embodies the philosophy of reducing excess and embracing minimalism by separating oneself from the physical and mental clutter, promoting a lifestyle of simplicity and focus.

examples.chinese: 练习断舍离(練習斷捨離)，可以让你的生活更简单(可以讓你的生活更簡單)。
examples.pinyin: Liànxí duànshělí, kěyǐ ràng nǐ de shēnghuó gèng jiǎndān.
examples.english: Practicing decluttering can make your life simpler.

examples.chinese: 我决定开始断舍离(我決定開始斷捨離)，因为家里东西太多了(因為家裡東西太多了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ juédìng kāishǐ duànshělí, yīnwèi jiālǐ dōngxi tài duō le.
examples.english: I decided to start decluttering because there are too many things at home.

examples.chinese: 断舍离的过程(斷捨離的過程)让人感到轻松(讓人感到輕鬆)。
examples.pinyin: Duànshělí de guòchéng ràng rén gǎndào qīngsōng.
examples.english: The process of decluttering makes people feel relaxed.
